title: Hymenophyllum trichophorum
intro: Hymenophyllum trichophorum, also known as the net veined filmy fern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds and net-like venation. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Hymenophyllum trichophorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to relieve symptoms of cough, cold, and fever.
taxonomy: Hymenophyllum trichophorum is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This classification places it within the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all ferns. As a member of the Hymenophyllaceae family, Hymenophyllum trichophorum is further grouped under the order Hymenophyllales. The family Hymenophyllaceae is a part of the class Polypodiopsida, which comprises leptosporangiate ferns. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other ferns within the same group.
